title: Apple shows how to run local agentic AI on Mac with MLX
friendlyTitle: Apple explains local AI agents on Mac
summary: Apple’s WWDC session demonstrates a local agentic AI stack using MLX, MLX-LM, and an OpenAI-compatible local server on Mac. The workflow lets coding agents and developer tools call local models, use tools, and keep inference on-device.
friendlySummary: Apple showed how developers can run agent-style AI workflows on a Mac using MLX and a local OpenAI-compatible server. The model can stay on the device while the agent uses tools.
whyItMatters: Local agents are attractive for privacy, cost control, low latency, and offline work. For developers and power users, Apple’s guidance makes on-device agent workflows feel less experimental and more like a real deployment pattern.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This matters for privacy and cost. Some people and teams may prefer agents that work locally instead of sending code or data to a cloud model.
